Results for VDBA Scaling Analysis

O.W.

2025-11-10

Methods

  • 37 species datasets of raw tri-axial accelerometer data.
  • Formatted to standard data structure
  • Smoothed outliers with a butterworth filter (cutoff_freq, 0.5 Hz, to remove only sudden spikes)
  • Generated dynamic VDBA by removing static acceleration (either as a rolling average of 10 seconds of VDBA for continuous data or as the average of the burst)
  • Separated instances into active and inactive categories such that all instances with a rolling standard deviation (measure of flatness in the rolling window) in the botton 25% were classed as inactive.
  • Summarised max and mean VDBA per individual.

Individual Analysis

Where the masses of the exact individual are known. Per species plot and then all known individuals on the same graph.

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: log10(meanVDBA) ~ LogMass * threshold + (1 | Dataset)
##    Data: all_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: -122.5
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -5.7012 -0.3753 -0.0460  0.5687  3.7104 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Dataset  (Intercept) 0.33015  0.5746  
##  Residual             0.03178  0.1783  
## Number of obs: 297, groups:  Dataset, 7
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                            Estimate Std. Error        df t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                -0.05814    0.41580  49.73709  -0.140 0.889365    
## LogMass                    -0.09753    0.08469 253.18349  -1.152 0.250577    
## thresholdall               -0.05849    0.15114 285.05298  -0.387 0.699061    
## thresholdinactive          -1.77495    0.15114 285.05298 -11.744  < 2e-16 ***
## LogMass:thresholdall       -0.14240    0.03690 285.05298  -3.859 0.000141 ***
## LogMass:thresholdinactive   0.18625    0.03690 285.05298   5.047 8.01e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##                (Intr) LogMss thrshldl thrshldn LgMss:thrshldl
## LogMass        -0.851                                        
## thresholdll    -0.182  0.215                                 
## thrshldnctv    -0.182  0.215  0.500                          
## LgMss:thrshldl  0.179 -0.218 -0.986   -0.493                 
## LgMss:thrshldn  0.179 -0.218 -0.493   -0.986    0.500
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: log10(maxVDBA) ~ LogMass + (1 | Dataset)
##    Data: active_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: -125.5
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.5367 -0.3200  0.0440  0.4525  2.9570 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Dataset  (Intercept) 0.08748  0.2958  
##  Residual             0.01177  0.1085  
## Number of obs: 99, groups:  Dataset, 7
## 
## Fixed effects:
##              Estimate Std. Error        df t value Pr(>|t|)   
## (Intercept)  0.962656   0.342409 40.440215   2.811  0.00757 **
## LogMass      0.004046   0.077409 58.041208   0.052  0.95850

## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##         (Intr)
## LogMass -0.944

Single system analysis

Where exact masses are not included (species averages) but all data is collected using the same accelerometer sampling protocol.Repeated for all Clemente datasets and for all Buchmann datasets. # V1: Axivity (Clemente)

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: log10(meanVDBA) ~ LogMass * threshold + (1 | Dataset)
##    Data: axivity_summaries
##

## Scaled residuals:

## Number of obs: 207, groups:  Dataset, 10
## 
## Fixed effects:

## LogMass                    -0.20580    0.17300   8.40734  -1.190   0.2667

## thresholdinactive          -1.80616    0.23973 192.48945  -7.534 1.87e-12 ***

## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##                (Intr) LogMss thrshldl thrshldn LgMss:thrshldl
## LogMass        -0.984                                        
## thresholdll    -0.173  0.172                                 
## thrshldnctv    -0.173  0.172  0.500                          
## LgMss:thrshldl  0.171 -0.173 -0.992   -0.496                 
## LgMss:thrshldn  0.171 -0.173 -0.496   -0.992    0.500

All species pooled

All species, with approximate/average masses.

## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Name', 'threshold'. You can override using
## the `.groups` argument.
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
